name: JFK International Airport
description: John F. Kennedy International Airport in Queens, New York. American Airlines terminal for transatlantic flights.

name: Milan Malpensa Airport
description: Milan's main international airport, gateway to Northern Italy and the Alps. Flight AA0198 arrives here from JFK.
address: Via dell'Aeroporto, 21013 Casorate Sempione VA, Italy

name: Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
description: A legendary alpine resort town at the foot of Mont Blanc in the French Alps. Stunning glaciers, world-class skiing, and a vibrant mountain town atmosphere.

name: Hotel Lykke Chamonix Spa
description: The group hotel in Chamonix. Nordic-inspired spa hotel in the heart of the town, steps from the main street and cable car stations.

name: Aiguille du Midi
description: A 3,842-meter peak above Chamonix reached by the world's highest vertical ascent cable car. Panoramic views of Mont Blanc and the entire Mont Blanc Massif — one of the most spectacular viewpoints in Europe.

name: Centre des Congrès de Chamonix
description: Chamonix's main convention and performance center. Used for rehearsals during the tour's first days in the Alps.

name: Martigny Place Centrale
description: The central square of Martigny, Switzerland — a charming open-air concert setting in the Valais region. Concert #1 takes place here at 8pm on July 29.

name: Pont du Gard
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and the best-preserved ancient Roman aqueduct in the world, built in the 1st century AD. Three tiers of arches span the Gardon River near Nîmes. An unmissable stop in Provence.
address: 400 Route du Pont du Gard, 30210 Vers-Pont-du-Gard, France

name: Arles
description: A UNESCO-listed city in Provence famous for its remarkably well-preserved Roman monuments, Van Gogh's most iconic paintings created here, and a vibrant arts scene. The Roman arena still hosts events today.

name: Ibis Styles Arles Palais du Congrès
description: The group hotel in Arles, located near the Palais des Congrès convention center.
address: 2 Rue de la Cavalerie, 13200 Arles, France

name: Les Baux-de-Provence
description: One of the most dramatic villages in France, perched on a rocky outcrop with a ruined medieval citadel overlooking the Alpilles. Stunning panoramic views, ancient streets, and the famous Carrières de Lumières light show nearby.

name: Château de La Tour d'Aigues
description: A dramatic Renaissance château in the Luberon, partially ruined but majestically restored for cultural events. Concert #2 takes place here at 9pm on July 31 — an extraordinary outdoor setting.
address: Place du Château, 84240 La Tour-d'Aigues, France

name: Le Grau-du-Roi Beach
description: A laid-back Mediterranean beach town at the mouth of the Rhône delta, about an hour from Arles. Wide sandy beaches, seafood restaurants, and a quintessential southern French coastal atmosphere.

name: Avignon
description: The 'City of Popes' — a walled medieval city on the Rhône River with the magnificent Palais des Papes (Palace of the Popes), the famous Pont Saint-Bénézet bridge, and a UNESCO-listed historic center.

name: Palais des Papes
description: The largest Gothic palace in the world, built in the 14th century when Avignon was the seat of Western Christianity. A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most important medieval monuments in Europe.

name: Pont Saint-Bénézet (Pont d'Avignon)
description: The famous medieval bridge of Avignon, immortalized in the French folk song 'Sur le pont d'Avignon.' Only four of the original 22 arches remain, jutting dramatically into the Rhône.

name: Place de l'Horloge, Avignon
description: Avignon's lively main square, fronted by the Town Hall and the opera house. One of two possible Concert #3 venues on August 2.

name: Marseille O Beach
description: A popular beach club on the Marseille waterfront — good stop for lunch between Arles and Nice.

name: Nice
description: The capital of the French Riviera — a dazzling Mediterranean city of colorful architecture, a pebble beach promenade, an irresistible old town, and one of the great food markets in France.

name: NH Hotel Nice
description: The group hotel in Nice, centrally located for exploring the Riviera.

name: Vieux-Nice (Old Nice)
description: Nice's old town — a labyrinth of narrow lanes, baroque churches, vivid ochre and pink facades, and the famous Cours Saleya flower and food market. A sensory feast. The guided tour ends at the market.

name: Cours Saleya Market
description: One of the most beautiful open-air markets in France, held daily along a grand boulevard in Old Nice. Flowers, Provençal produce, cheese, olives, and socca — the famous Nice chickpea pancake.

name: Cassa Nissa
description: A beloved restaurant in Old Nice serving traditional Niçois cuisine — pissaladière, salade niçoise, socca, and pasta with pistou. The group lunch spot on August 4.

name: Nice Bandstand / Antibes / St-Raphaël
description: Concert #4 on August 4 — final venue TBD among Nice Bandstand, Antibes, or St-Raphaël. All are beautiful Riviera settings for an evening open-air performance.

name: Hotel Villa Borghi
description: A beautiful lakeside villa hotel in Northern Italy for the tour's final night — pool, grounds, and a farewell dinner before the flight home.
address: Via per Varese, 6, 21020 Azzate VA, Italy

name: La Kiuva
description: A traditional Valle d'Aosta mountain restaurant en route from Milan to Chamonix. Welcome lunch stop for the group on arrival day.

name: Restaurant La Madone
description: Restaurant for group dinner before Concert #2 on July 31 in the Provence region.

name: Saint-Paul-de-Vence
description: One of the most visited medieval villages in France — a perfectly preserved hilltop town with ramparts, art galleries, and stunning views over the Riviera. Home to the Fondation Maeght modern art museum.

name: Roman Arena of Arles (Amphithéâtre)
description: A 1st-century Roman amphitheatre seating 20,000, still used today for bullfights and concerts. One of the best-preserved Roman arenas in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Van Gogh Sites & Foundation, Arles
description: Arles is where Van Gogh lived and painted over 300 works, including The Yellow Room and Starry Night Over the Rhône. The Fondation Vincent van Gogh Arles has rotating exhibitions, and a walking trail marks his painting locations.
address: 35 Rue du Docteur Fanton, 13200 Arles, France
